commit.message:
fix: resolve all security vulnerabilities via pnpm overrides

- Pin brace-expansion@<1.1.13 to exact 1.1.13 (not >=1.1.13 which resolved to 5.x)
- Pin brace-expansion@>=2.0.0 <2.0.3 to 2.0.3
- Pin picomatch@>=4.0.0 <4.0.4 to 4.0.4
- Add path-based overrides: anymatch>picomatch, micromatch>picomatch, readdirp>picomatch
- Pin yaml@>=2.0.0 <2.8.3 to 2.8.3 (Stack Overflow fix)
- Pin flatted, node-forge to exact safe versions
Result: 0 vulnerabilities (was 13 high/moderate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve risk score calculation for identity-based risk factors

- Lower digital footprint threshold (2+ platforms vs 10+)
- Add scaled risk weights based on platform count
- Add public profile information exposure risk
- Add username correlation risk detection
- Lower cluster confidence threshold (0.7 vs 0.8)
- Add potential linkage detection for any clusters

These changes ensure risk scores reflect OSINT findings even without
file exposures, which is the primary use case for defense.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: replace O(n) IIFE turn search in Timeline with memoized binary search

The current-turn lookup was an inline IIFE that ran on every render, doing a
linear scan through all turns. During playback currentTime updates at animation
speed (~60fps), so this ran hundreds of times per second on large sessions.

Fix: extract into useMemo keyed on [currentTime, turns]. Since turns are
time-ordered and non-overlapping, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of
linear scan. Falls back to last turn when currentTime is past all turn ranges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: remove autonomy grants section from public-facing instructions

Auto-push to main, auto-delete files, and auto-run commands are appropriate
for a trusted solo dev context but not safe defaults for a public repo where
contributors' AI agents may read these instructions.

- Added Icon registry rule: both lucide-react import AND ICON_MAP entry are required.

  with absolutely-positioned dropdowns. Documents the AppHeader fix pattern with
  position:relative + z.active on container, z.tooltip on dropdown.
- Updated Review Checklist to match all three new rules.

fix: proxy `/api` routes to backend in Vite dev mode (#21)

* fix: add Vite proxy for /api/* routes in dev mode

Vite dev server returned 404 for all /api/* routes (Coach, sessions,
config, apply, streaming) because no proxy was configured to forward
requests to the backend server (server.js on port 4242).

- Add proxy config in vite.config.js forwarding /api/* to port 4242
- Update CLAUDE.md and README.md to document two-server dev workflow

Closes #20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: note port 4242 must be free for Vite proxy to connect

The backend (bin/agentviz.js) auto-selects a free port starting from
4242. If another process holds 4242 the backend binds to 4243+, but
the Vite proxy is hardcoded to 4242 and will fail to connect.

A doc-only approach was chosen over an env-var escape hatch to avoid
introducing new runtime behavior and keep the risk of new bugs at zero.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract DEFAULT_API_PORT to shared config.js

Move the hardcoded port 4242 into a root-level config.js imported by
vite.config.js and bin/agentviz.js. This eliminates the port mismatch
risk where the backend could bind to a different port than Vite proxies
to. Both now read from the same source of truth.

Removes the port-must-be-free doc warnings added in the previous commit
since the shared constant makes them unnecessary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: simplify dev setup notes in CLAUDE.md and README.md

Remove config.js implementation detail from user-facing docs. The port
is already visible in the command listings; the internal wiring is not
needed to run the project.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: flatten Store MSIX artifact and update manifest in store-submit (#50)

Three fixes for Store submission using wrong package versions:

1. Flatten Store MSIX artifact - copy .msix files to a flat directory
   before uploading so the msstore CLI can find them via -i flag
2. Add build-and-test dependency to store-submit job so it has access
   to the version output
3. Update Package.appxmanifest version and publisher in the store-submit
   checkout before running msstore publish, ensuring the CLI reads
   correct metadata even if it analyzes the project

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(sandbox): move venv to overlay fs to avoid host arch conflict

Set UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/home/agent/.venv in the Dockerfile so the
sandbox venv lives on the overlay filesystem, not the virtiofs-mounted
workspace. This avoids two problems:
- Host .venv (different arch) being picked up inside the sandbox
- Cross-device hardlink failures causing nvidia WHEEL tag churn

Also update copilot-instructions.md with sandbox quickstart guide so
agents can auto-detect and configure the sandbox environment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: save and restore proxy vars in k8s_cluster fixture

The previous fix permanently unset HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY for the entire
test session. This is safe in CI (GHA runners have no proxy vars) and in
the Docker sandbox (HF models are pre-cached), but would break any
subsequent test that needs the proxy — e.g. a developer with a corporate
proxy running pipeline e2e tests that fall back to downloading HF models.

The k8s client's urllib3 pool manager captures proxy settings at creation
time, so it's safe to restore the vars after _k8s_config_local() runs.
Save the proxy vars before unsetting, restore them after yield so the rest
of the session sees the original environment unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: unset proxy vars in k8s_cluster fixture for sandbox compatibility

The Python kubernetes client's urllib3 pool manager routes all HTTPS
traffic through HTTPS_PROXY (including localhost connections to k3s at
port 6443). The Docker sandbox sets HTTPS_PROXY=http://host.docker.internal:3128
and the proxy server rejects forwarding to localhost, causing all k8s
functional tests to fail with ProxyError.

Fix by unsetting HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/https_proxy/http_proxy at the
start of the session-scoped k8s_cluster fixture, before any kubernetes
client configuration is loaded.

Also update .sandbox/README.md to document that the conftest now handles
this automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Pattern Detector (Phase 3)

After 20+ trades, Coach AI automatically detects patterns and displays insights:

1. Emotion & Discipline Analysis:
   - Identifies which emotional states lead to undisciplined trading
   - Alerts when discipline rate drops below 60% in specific emotions

2. Dangerous Sequences Detection:
   - Detects current losing streaks (2+ consecutive losses)
   - Warns about max loss streaks in last 20 trades
   - Suggests personal limits (stop after 2 losses)

3. Best Trading Conditions:
   - Finds markets with highest win rate (60%+ threshold)
   - Correlates positive emotions with winning trades
   - Shows optimal conditions for performance

4. Revenge Trading Detection:
   - Identifies patterns of trading after losses on same day
   - Flags trades with revenge/impulsive/FOMO emotions
   - Warns if 3+ revenge trades detected

Insights display in dashboard with visual cards:
- Warning cards (red) for alerts
- Success cards (green) for strengths
- Info cards (purple) for patterns

commit.message:
feat: validate runtime command values in syu.yaml at config load time (#88)

Reject runtimes.python.command and runtimes.node.command values that
look like shell expressions.  The validator rejects any value that:
- Contains shell metacharacters (; | & $ ` ! () {} <> ' " \)
- Contains whitespace (spaces or newlines)
- Is an absolute path that does not point to an existing file

'auto' (and empty string) is always accepted.  Bare executable names
such as 'python3', 'node', 'bun' pass through unchanged; PATH search
happens later in resolve_runtime_command.

The error is surfaced with context pointing to the syu.yaml path so
users can identify and fix the offending value immediately.

Closes #68

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[analysis] Fix breathing-mode infrasound-size dimensional error (sqrt)

breathing_mode_infrasound_size() computed R = 3*K_f/(rho_f*omega^2) which
has units m^2, not m.  Corrected to R = sqrt(3*K_f/(rho_f*omega^2)),
changing the punchline from ~410 m to ~20 m.  The qualitative conclusion
(breathing mode is never infrasonic for any real organism) is unchanged.

- Fix sqrt in src/analytical/dimensional_analysis.py
- Update paper3-scaling-laws/main.tex: 410 m -> 20 m (abstract + Section 5)
- Fix 'machine precision' -> 'algebraic consistency' in paper1 figure caption
- Add 3 regression tests for the corrected formula
- All 183 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Address Reviewer A M2/M3: clinical displacement quantification and pelvic BC analysis

Expanded Clinical Implications (M2):
- Added displacement quantification: 55 um at resonance, 170 um sub-resonant
- Compared to filling (19 mm) and micturition (30 mm) displacements
- Computed equivalent pressure (0.7 cmH2O) vs Adelta threshold (5-15 cmH2O)
- Key insight: WBV strain rate (0.1/s) is 500x faster than filling (2e-4/s)
- Added PIEZO2 mechanotransduction pathway (Marshall 2020, Nature)
- Three clinical scenarios: vehicle urgency, stress incontinence, long-drive
- Added references: Marshall2020, Dalghi2021, Fowler2008, Habler1993

Expanded Pelvic Constraint (M3):
- Quantitative estimate: 15-25% frequency increase with 40% rigid contact
- Shifted range: 16-21 Hz (conservative lower bound without constraint)
- Mode shape modification: asymmetric modes on free dome
- Clinical interpretation: reinforces sub-resonant WBV response

Updated conclusion with displacement context and PIEZO2 pathway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Apply Reviewer A minor revision to Paper 3 (scaling laws)

Address all 11 suggestions from Reviewer A:

Major (5):
1. Reframe intro: pivot from technique to physical discovery (quasi-universal Pi_0 ~ 0.07)
2. Expand Table 2: show all Pi-groups (h/a, c/a, E, rho_w/rho_f) for each species
3. Define lambda_n explicitly with closed-form expression (Eq. 8)
4. Add sensitivity subsection: membrane dominates (78%), h/a is key Pi-group
5. Broader impact paragraph: blast injury, medical ultrasound, vehicle NVH, underwater acoustics

Minor (6):
6. Fix abstract equation consistency: remove 1/(2pi) from scaling law (absorbed into Phi_n)
7. Replace 'machine precision' with 'algebraic consistency' throughout
8. Highlight 3: note 1/a scaling holds only when other Pi-groups are approximately constant
9. Title: 'From Rats to Humans' -> 'Predictions Across Mammalian Scales'
10. Section 5 title: 'always ultrasonic' -> 'never infrasonic'
11. Add references: Warburton (1976), Junger (1952), West et al. (1997), Abramson (1966), Fahy & Gardonio (2007)

[infra] Stop tracking generated PDFs/PNGs to reduce LFS bandwidth

Removed 93 binary files from git index (kept locally). These are all
regenerable: draft snapshots, compiled PDFs, generated figures.
Style-reference PDFs remain tracked (not regenerable).

Going forward, .gitignore prevents accidental re-addition.

commit.message:
[audit] Cross-paper consistency audit: 3 critical, 7 warnings, 4 info

Audited all four papers (Paper 1 brown note, Paper 2 gas pockets,
Paper 3 scaling laws, Bladder resonance) for parameter consistency,
cross-references, notation, coupling ratio definitions, and code-paper
agreement.

Critical findings:
- Paper 2 lacks formal citation of Paper 1 (no bib entry)
- Bladder paper has 3 missing figure files
- Paper 1 Section 2 coupling ratio range (10^3-10^4) conflicts with
  Section 4 computed value (6.6x10^4)

All shared parameters, derived quantities, and code outputs verified
consistent within rounding tolerance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[bladder] Deepen parametric analysis (M1) and add sensitivity/UQ (M4) (#87)

Reviewer A M1: Stiffness/mass decomposition
- Add stiffness_decomposition() decomposing K_bend, K_memb, K_P, m_wall, m_fluid
- At V*=169 mL: K_P=67%, K_memb=32%, K_bend<1%; m_fluid=87%, m_wall=13%
- Derive df2/dV=0 condition: d(ln K)/dV = d(ln m)/dV; crossing at 168 mL
- New figure: fig_stiffness_mass_decomposition (4-panel PDF)

Reviewer A M4: Sensitivity and UQ analysis
- One-at-a-time sensitivity: E dominates (183%), h secondary (14%), rest <1%
- Minimum-shift analysis: V* robust to all params except E (169->461 mL)
- New figures: fig_tornado_sensitivity, fig_minimum_shift (PDF)
- Updated results.tex with new subsections 3.2 and 3.3

Also:
- make_bladder_model now accepts **overrides for parameter sweeps
- 27 new tests (all passing) in tests/test_bladder_m1m4.py
- 161 existing tests still pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify Linux build with conditional net472 and matrix CI (#563)

Enable `dotnet test` in the root of the repo/for all projects. It didn't
work before because our `net472` can't run on Linux.
- Centralize `TargetFrameworks` in `test/Directory.Build.props`
- Set `IsTestProject=false` in `test/Directory.Build.targets` when
building `net472` on Linux.

Change `build.yml` to use a matrix strategy instead of the separate,
near identical os-specific jobs.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Oleg Sych <olegsych@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[analysis] Add mode transition map to borborygmi model

- mode_transition_map(): computes all 5 mode frequencies across 0.1-100 mL
  volume range with log spacing, identifies dominant mode at each volume,
  detects crossover volumes where dominant mode changes
- plot_mode_transition_map(): publication-quality log-log figure with
  clinical band shading, crossover markers, and dominant-mode background
- 19 new tests: monotonicity, crossover detection, clinical overlap,
  volume-independence of radial mode, stiff-wall physics, figure smoke test
- Generated centrepiece figure for paper2-gas-pockets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[analysis] Fix bladder E/mu confusion, thin-shell caveat, constraint direction

Reviewer B F1: Convert Nenadic shear moduli to Young's (E = 2.98*mu).
  E_min: 10 -> 29 kPa, E_max: 200 -> 145 kPa.
  f2 min: 12 Hz at 170 mL -> 13.5 Hz at 222 mL.
  Coupling ratio at 300 mL: 7600 -> 6500.

Reviewer B F2: Add h/R column to Table 2, mark entries with h/R > 0.1
  (50 mL, 100 mL) with dagger. Add thin-shell validity caveat in theory.

Reviewer B M2: Pelvic constraints RAISE frequencies (Rayleigh's theorem),
  not lower them. Free-shell model is a lower bound on f2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
